_source: GDC Vault, Gamasutra, Game Developer magazine research
_note: Long-tail game design data. Deep mechanics, not surface descriptions.

coyote_time.description: Allow jump input 6-10 frames after walking off ledge
coyote_time.why: Reduces frustration; player 'feels' like they jumped on time even when they didn't

jump_buffering.description: Queue jump input 6-8 frames before landing
jump_buffering.why: Responsive feel; player can press jump early and it triggers on land

variable_jump_height.description: Hold longer = jump higher; release early = cut short
variable_jump_height.implementation: Cut y-velocity by 50% on button release if still ascending

jump_physics.coyote_and_buffer_stack: Both can be active simultaneously; industry standard since 2010s

gravity_scale.why: Snappier feel; less floaty; Celeste approach

movement.air_control: Reduce horizontal accel 40-60% in air vs ground; still controllable but weighty
movement.deceleration_ground: Faster decel than accel feels responsive (Super Mario Bros model)
movement.turn_friction: Brief slide when reversing direction; adds momentum feel

dash.momentum_preservation: Dash velocity adds to existing velocity (Celeste) vs replaces it

level_design_rules.difficulty_curve: Introduce mechanic safely → challenge with mechanic → combine mechanics
level_design_rules.safe_zone_frequency: Every 45-90 seconds of gameplay; checkpoint or safe area

camera.leading_space: Show more space ahead of player in movement direction
camera.vertical_tracking: Smooth lerp up; snap down (players look down rarely)
camera.typical_zone: Player at 35% of screen width in movement direction

roguelike_roguelite.core_loop: Run → Die → Permanent upgrade → Better run

roguelike_vs_roguelite.roguelike: True permadeath; no persistent progress; complex; NetHack/Caves of Qud
roguelike_vs_roguelite.roguelite: Persistent meta-progression unlocks; broad audience; Hades/Dead Cells

run_pacing.power_spike_design: Player should feel overpowered by final boss area

build_diversity.synergy_depth: Good: A + B = 200% effectiveness. Great: A + B + C = 500% (emergent combinations)
build_diversity.minimum_viable_builds: 10-15 distinct viable builds prevents solution being 'solved'
build_diversity.dead_synergies: Avoid: items that conflict and produce nothing; player feels cheated

procedural_generation.room_templates_needed: Minimum 30 per biome to avoid repetition fatigue
procedural_generation.enemy_mix_algorithm: 1 elite + 2-4 fodder per room is standard combat density

successful_examples.hades: Best in class narrative roguelite; 300+ voiced reactions to events
successful_examples.dead_cells: Blueprint unlock system; daily runs; tight combat
successful_examples.slay_the_spire: Card game roguelite; 3 characters; infinite build depth

turn_based.atb_active_time: Bars fill in real-time; attack when full; Final Fantasy model
turn_based.initiative_order: Speed stat determines turn order; faster characters act more often
turn_based.action_economy: Number of actions per turn; most important balance lever

action_rpg.lock_on: Z-target (Zelda) or soft lock (Soulslike); 30-45 degree tracking arc
action_rpg.stamina_system: Resource for attack, dodge, block; prevents button mashing
action_rpg.i_frames: Invincibility during dodge roll (typically 0.2-0.3s); critical for Soulslike feel
action_rpg.guard_break: Chip damage through block forces aggressive play

level_scaling.flat_scaling: Each level adds fixed stat amount; simple but breaks late game math
level_scaling.percentage_scaling: Each level adds percentage; compounds, can inflate stats absurdly
level_scaling.diminishing_returns: Best: each point of stat gives less benefit; healthy long-term

skill_trees.binary_vs_sliding: Binary: unlock ability. Sliding: 1/3/5 points for increasing effect
skill_trees.depth_vs_breadth: Deep narrow tree → specialist builds. Wide shallow → generalist
skill_trees.locked_branches: Require investment before unlocking; focuses player identity

narrative_design.companion_system: Approval rating changes dialogue; Bioware model (Dragon Age, Mass Effect)
narrative_design.dialogue_trees: Player choice should have meaningful consequences, not just tone choice
narrative_design.quest_design: Main + 2-3 side activities per zone; avoid checklist quests (collect 10 items)

fast_ttk.examples: Call of Duty; emphasizes mechanical aim skill

recoil_design.random_recoil: Unpredictable; lower skill ceiling; casual appeal
recoil_design.pattern_recoil: CS2 model; learnable; high skill ceiling; spray control mastery

feel_and_feedback.hit_feedback: Screen shake + controller rumble + hitmarker + sound cue + damage number; all 5 amplify satisfaction
feel_and_feedback.footstep_audio: Critical for competitive; directional audio 3D positioning

map_design.three_lane_design: Left/Mid/Right paths; classic Counter-Strike model; clear positional warfare
map_design.cover_density: Enough cover to punish peaks; not so much it's impossible to clear
map_design.sightlines: Mix of long (rifle) and short (shotgun/SMG) angles; weapon diversity
map_design.spawn_protection: 5-8 seconds safe from enemy; time to reach first position

survival_craft.resource_loop: Gather → Craft → Explore → Gather (richer resources) → Craft (better tools)

hunger_thirst_mechanics.grace_period: 30+ minutes before first hunger critical; tutorial period
hunger_thirst_mechanics.regeneration_incentive: Eating should restore HP, not just prevent death penalty
hunger_thirst_mechanics.food_variety_benefit: Minecraft's saturation system: diverse diet lasts longer

base_building.snap_system: Pieces snap to grid; reduces friction; Valheim model
base_building.structural_integrity: Unsupported structures collapse; adds challenge; Rust/Valheim model
base_building.raid_protection: Offline raid protection in survival MMOs critical for retention

progression_gating.tech_tree: Clear next-goal always visible; player is never lost on what to build next
progression_gating.boss_gates: Boss drops unlock tier; milestone markers; Terraria model
